summary: es6-codify exfiltrates process.env to hardcoded remote host on require
details: The package's main module invokes a `log()` function at top level on module load. This POSTs a JSON payload containing the full `process.env`, `process.cwd()`, `process.argv`, and the Node version to the hardcoded host `jhyugdawjdxlkanm.casa` at path `/post-d`. The behavior fires whenever a consumer `require()`s or `import`s `es6-codify`, so any build or CI step that pulls this package leaks its entire environment — typically including credentials such as NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS_*, and other CI secrets — to an attacker-controlled destination. The `.casa` host with a random subdomain has no relation to the package's advertised purpose (a generic ES6 string/array/object utility helper); the utility-library framing is a lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
